## Operation

- `listWorkspaceAssignedAgentProfilesV2` `GET /v2/client/workspaces/{workspace_id}/ai-agent/tasks/assigned-agent-profiles` -> `200 AssignedAgentProfileMapResponse`; generated path `v2.aiAgent.tasks.assignedAgentProfiles`; scenarios `2`

- Scenario names: `component id keyed assigned profile map`, `historical threads are omitted`

## Read-model rules

- server: include only currently active queued/running/stopping AI Agent assignments in the workspace map
- server: omit completed, stopped, failed, unassigned, and historical-only task threads from the map
- server: use the actual Riido component_id/task_id string as the map key without renaming it to a literal component_id field
- server: because a task/subtask can have only one active AI Agent assignment, each component_id maps to one AssignedAgentProfile value
- profile: avatar_url is copied from the assigned agent's profile_thumbnail_url when present
- profile: tmp_color is the fixture-provided avatar fallback color copied into fixture-created agents; direct-created agents may omit it
- client: use this query for list/card avatar hints, not for participant dropdown candidate selection or task-thread history

## Schemas

- `AssignedAgentProfileMapResponse`: `schema_version`, `workspace_id`, `assigned_agent_profiles`
- `AssignedAgentProfile`: `avatar_url`, `tmp_color`

## Invariant anchors

- assigned_agent_profiles is keyed by the exact component_id/task_id string.
- only queued/running/stopping AI Agent assignments appear in the map.
- completed, stopped, failed, unassigned, and historical-only threads are omitted.
- the query is for list/card avatar hints, not participant dropdown candidates or task-thread history.

## Evidence Loop

- Observe: Workspace assigned-agent profile map meaning was split across policy prose and API projection prose while DSL policy, schemas, scenarios, and OpenAPI already defined the read model.
- Hypothesis: A focused verifier can make assigned-profile-map drift observable and keep task/card avatar hints separate from participant dropdown and thread history APIs.
- Execute: Verify the v2 query operation, generated path, response schemas, read-model policy rules, DSL/IR tuple parity, OpenAPI projection, and active-only map semantics.
- Evaluate: CI fails when map key semantics, active-only filtering rules, schema fields, generated reader, or v2 OpenAPI projection drift.
- Retrospective: Future assigned-profile list/card semantics should update API policy/schema fixtures and this manifest before reader text changes.
